original_hypothesis.title: Dead-end state: LockingMultiRewards has no user-accessible unlock path (operator-only), creating practical fund freeze if ops automation fails
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: Only operators can call processExpiredLocks, and users have no self-service method to unlock expired locks. If the operator set is misconfigured, removed, censored, or simply stops running (automation outage), users’ locked staking tokens remain locked indefinitely even after unlockTime. This is an operational single point of failure that can freeze user funds.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: While it depends on off-chain operations, it is a high-impact, realistic production incident mode. Phase A documentation explicitly states reliance on Gelato automation to release locks.
deployment_assumptions: Operators are not guaranteed to be live/available forever (Gelato task, bot infra, key rotation)
No alternative emergency operator or user escape hatch exists
original_hypothesis.config_evidence: Docs: 'relies on an off-chain Gelato task to release user locks'; code: processExpiredLocks is onlyOperators and there is no user unlock function.

proof_of_signal.pattern: processExpiredLocks is onlyOperators; no public function exists for users to unlock their own expired locks

preconditions: No operator successfully calls processExpiredLocks for that user

initial_operational_evidence.suspicious_workflow: core user fund movement requires off-chain automation; no fallback
initial_operational_evidence.admin_dependency: owner must manage operator set; operators must actively process

operational_questions: How many independent operators exist and what are their runbooks/SLAs?
Is there an emergency procedure to add a new operator quickly?
Are users notified/able to request processing?
validation_requirements: Review deployment ops: who is set as operator(s) and how keys are managed
Simulate operator outage and measure user funds stuck duration
Assess whether pause/unpause affects ability to process expired locks

preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_1: Trigger or benefit from operator outage (e.g., targeted chain congestion not needed; just downtime)
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_2: Users cannot unlock; funds remain illiquid
preliminary_attack_methodology.phase_3: Secondary impacts: panic, forced OTC discounts, protocol reputational loss

potential_operational_impact.operational_disruption: locked user funds become stuck; protocol-wide incident
potential_operational_impact.recovery_difficulty: medium (requires restoring operator service or changing operator set)

investigation_priorities.on_chain_signals: no processExpiredLocks calls for long periods; growing expired locks backlog
investigation_priorities.operational_anomalies: increasing support tickets; operator tx failures
investigation_priorities.behavioral_patterns: operator calls revert due to ErrInvalidLockIndex ordering constraint

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
